108.status: Exited in the first inning after striking out in his first at-bat March 11. Was replaced at shortstop in the bottom of the first for precautionary reasons.
Injured on a throw to the infield on Feb. 25. Believes he'll be ready for Opening Day. Faced live pitching on March 11 and nearing playing in Minor League games before return to Cactus League action.
Underwent labrum surgery on May 14. Has been throwing bullpens without any issues with his latest one coming on March 10, but appears likely to start the season on the injured list.

Diagnosed on Jan. 31 but was able to throw and eat normally while dealing with fatigue. Behind the other pitchers, but has been throwing bullpens with no issues and will progress to facing hitters next.

Dealt with nerve issue and thoracic outlet syndrome symptoms in the offseason after ending 2025 on the injured list but believes he can manage it. Faced hitters on March 6 and said he expects to be ready for Opening Day.

110.status: Threw first bullpen session on March 10. However, still has a low probability of being ready for Opening Day.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Felt soreness in right elbow while rehabbing right oblique injury. After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return yet.

112.status: Manager Craig Counsell noted on March 8 that Hodge will be shut down from throwing for a couple weeks before being re-evaluated. The righty is expected to open the season on the IL.
Resumed playing catch on March 3 after pausing his throwing program for 10 days. Could return to a mound later this month, but expected to open the season on the IL.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.
Threw a bullpen session Feb. 27 and was medically cleared Feb. 28 by Dr. Keith Meister, who performed Steele's elbow surgery (left ulnar collateral ligament revision repair) on April 18, 2025.

113.status: Greene was scheduled to have arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from his right elbow. He is expected to miss 14-16 weeks.
Departed the game on March 6 after landing awkwardly while attempting to make a catch in the top of the eighth inning. An MRI exam revealed a low-grade sprain. Dunn could miss three to five days.

114.status: Continuing throwing progression at 90-120 feet. Team anticipates him throwing off a mound in the coming days. (Last updated: March 9)
Throwing bullpen sessions and from 90-120 feet on flat ground. Expected to progress to throwing live batting practice in second half of March and miss the start of '26 season. (Last updated: March 5)
Suffered moderate grade strain during Feb. 24 appearance against the Dodgers that is expected to sideline him for 6-8 weeks. (Last updated: Feb. 28)

116.status: Pitched in simulated game on March 10. (Updated March 10)
Placed on 60-day IL on March 10. Has not yet resumed throwing. Diagnosed with elbow inflammation after reporting general arm soreness and being examined by doctors. (Updated March 10)
Reported abdominal tightness after last outing on March 7. Will rehab daily. (Updated March 10)
Completing a hitting progression. Not yet throwing. (Updated March 10)
Threw a bullpen session on March 10. (Updated March 10)
Removed from Chinese Taipei roster for World Baseball Classic. Returned to Lakeland, Fla., on March 7 and underwent further tests on March 8. Optioned to Triple-A Toledo on March 9. (Updated March 9)
Playing catch out to 105 feet three times this week. Underwent Tommy John surgery on June 16. (Updated March 6)

116.playerName: Bryce Rainer (MLB No. 35 overall prospect; Tigers' No. 3)
116.status: Underwent season-ending surgery after dislocating his shoulder diving back to first base in a June 3 game for Single-A Lakeland. Continuing rehab process this Spring Training. Should be ready early in season. (Updated March 6)
Completing a return-to-play throwing progression after undergoing arthroscopic surgery on elbow this offseason. (Updated Feb. 24)

Underwent labrum repair surgery on right shoulder on Feb. 2, performed by Dr. Keith Meister, after offseason rehab program was unsuccessful. Expected to miss 2026 season but should be ready for Spring Training in 2027. (Updated Feb. 11)

118.status: Suffered Grade 1+ oblique strain on Feb. 25. Began playing catch again March 5 and is up to 60 feet. Will progress to next steps based on when symptoms cease.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Slow-played his offseason throwing program due to lingering discomfort. He could throw off the mound for the first time this spring during the week of March 9, manager Dave Roberts said.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in Stone's shoulder. He's shut down from throwing for at least a couple weeks, after which he'll be re-evaluated.
Underwent shoulder debridement procedure in September 2025. Has progressed to throwing full bullpen sessions.
Velocity is down from where it is expected to be, so the Dodgers are slow-playing his ramp-up. He is only playing catch.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025. Began throwing off the mound to a standing catcher in late February, with the goal of progressing to full bullpen sessions during March.

120.status: Began taking swings on March 10. Team wants to make sure he is healthy for Opening Day. (Last updated: March 10)
Continuing through throwing progression. (Last updated: March 4)
Began throwing progression and completed 25 throws at 60 feet. (Last updated: March 4)

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 80)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing arm care and throwing progression. (Last updated: Feb. 21)

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 54)
120.status: Threw 15 pitches at a distance of 60 feet on Feb. 20; continuing arm care and throwing progression. (Last updated: Feb. 21)

121.injury: Stress reaction in left hamate bone
121.status: Has been taking ground balls and batting cage swings while rehabbing his Feb. 11 surgery. Remains on track for Opening Day, but playing in Grapefruit League games is a “prerequisite,” per president of baseball operations David Stearns.

121.injury: Thoracic outlet syndrome in right shoulder
121.status: Surgery has been recommended for Stock after he was diagnosed with arterial thoracic outlet syndrome, per MLB Network insider Jon Heyman. Stock told Heyman that he hopes to pitch before the end of the season.
Was scratched from his scheduled Grapefruit League start on March 9 after reporting some shoulder fatigue. Will continue to throw in non-game action.
Made his first outing of the spring in a back-field Minor League scrimmage, throwing one scoreless inning on March 4. Hopes to appear in a Grapefruit League game before the end of March.

Kessinger suffered an injury on Feb. 27, and manager Carlos Mendoza announced March 1 that an MRI revealed “a pretty significant injury.” Surgery is “on the table,” according to Mendoza, who said Kessinger’s recovery time will be 8-12 weeks.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Continues to take at-bats on the Minor League side of camp as he builds towards a running progression. Could make his Cactus League debut next week.

134.status: Will continue to throw live BP as part of his rehab progression. Senior director of sports medicine Todd Tomczyk said Jones is meeting all the benchmarks. Goal is for Jones to be game-ready at the one-year mark post-surgery (May 21). (Last updated: Feb. 23)

Will not throw for a couple of weeks after experiencing swelling and soreness following a live workout on Feb. 22. Will miss playing for Team Venezuela in the World Baseball Classic, but the Pirates are hopeful he'll be ready by the start of the regular season. (Last updated: Feb. 23)

135.status: After missing the 2025 season, Musgrove returned to the mound for an exhibition against Team Great Britain. But he's being slow-played this spring, and the Padres have eased back his workload. It's unclear if he'll be built up in time to make the Opening Day roster. (Last updated: March 10)

Matsui threw his first bullpen session on March 6 after he sustained the injury during a live batting practice session in February. He's yet to face hitters, and his status for Opening Day is in question. (Last updated: March 9)

Waldron likely won't be built up enough to make the Opening Day roster. But he resumed throwing bullpens in early March, and manager Craig Stammen noted that he expects Waldron to return for an appearance or two in the Cactus League. (Last updated: March 9)

Adam, who underwent quadriceps surgery in September, has targeted Opening Day for a return. He faced hitters for the first time on Feb. 20 and began doing fielding drills in early March. (Last updated: March 9)

Brito underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. He was placed on the 60-day IL in February but has resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp. (Last updated: March 7)

Song exited the Padres' game on March 5 with lingering soreness stemming from an injury he sustained during the offseason. The Padres will be cautious, and Song's status for the Opening Day roster is unclear. (Last updated: March 6)

Canning, whose 2025 season ended in June because of the injury, threw a high-intensity bullpen session on a back field on March 3. His next step could be facing hitters, though he hasn't yet been cleared for fielding drills. (Last updated: March 3)

Hoeing was shut down after he felt discomfort in his throwing elbow during a live batting practice session in February. On March 2, he received a second opinion and, per manager Craig Stammen, was still weighing his next step. (Last updated: March 3)

Darvish underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. He will miss the 2026 season and has considered voiding the final three years of his contract. (Last updated: Jan. 27)

136.status: First full-effort bullpen cut short after discomfort during higher-intensity warmup pitches on March 11. Still cleared to play catch; will attempt another touch-and-feel bullpen in range of March 14-15. (Last updated: March 11) More »

136.expectedReturn: To the outfield, the week of March 16
136.status: Returned to lineup for first time in seven days on March 8 at designated hitter. Could progress to outfield again if all goes well. Had been experiencing fatigue in the same shoulder that he fractured last April. (Last updated: March 8) More »
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season. (Last updated: Jan. 30) More »

137.status: Hurt his hip while fielding a grounder in Team Colombia's exhibition game on March 3. Evaluated by team orthopedist Dr. Ken Akizuki on March 4 and was later diagnosed with a high grade strain of his right hip flexor. (Last updated: March 9)
Expected to see hitters soon and should be on the mound sometime in mid-March. (Last updated: March 9)
Re-injured his hamstring on March 2; an MRI revealed a Grade 2 strain, which will likely make him questionable for Opening Day. (Last updated: March 4)
Resumed hitting activities on March 4 but remains shut down from throwing. (Last updated: March 4)

Missed entire 2025 season due to left shoulder surgery and then underwent right knee arthroscopy to address his meniscus in August. Expected to continue bullpen progression at camp but is questionable for Opening Day. (Last updated: Feb. 27)
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Started a bullpen progression on Feb. 10 and is throwing twice per week. (Last updated: Feb. 16)
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister in Dallas on Sept. 24. Will begin a throwing progression in mid-March. (Last updated: Feb. 16)
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025. (Last updated: Feb. 15)

138.status: Was scheduled to start March 7, but was a late scratch. Team is giving it time for recovery to run its course, per manager Oliver Marmol. (Updated: March 8)

Dobbins is able to pitch but is being held out of agility drills. Underwent testing on his right knee recently and the results were what the Cardinals expected. Team is still mapping out the next steps of Dobbins' rehab. (Updated: Feb. 21)

Nootbaar underwent surgery on Oct. 7. He arrived at Cardinals camp on Feb. 18 and said there is no timetable for his return to game action. However, he has not yet been ruled out for Opening Day. (Updated: Feb. 19)
Thompson underwent surgery in August to clean up his left shoulder. He is on a throwing program, but he won't be cleared to pitch in games until some point in the regular season. (Updated: Feb. 13)

139.status: Withdrew from World Baseball Classic on Feb. 15, then “didn’t feel great” when playing catch Feb. 19. Exam revealed inflammation. Received a cortisone injection on Feb. 20 and played catch from 60 feet on Feb. 28. Has been playing catch regularly. (Last updated March 11)
Wilson has not pitched in Rays camp, as he is dealing with a back issue that required an injection. He is not expected to be ready for Opening Day. (Last updated Feb. 27)
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July. Placed on 60-day IL on Feb. 10. (Last updated Feb. 10)

140.status: Left a Feb. 23 game against the Angels with right hamstring tightness. Additional testing showed a strain and he will be down for around two weeks. (Last updated: Feb 28)
Felt back tightness on Feb. 25 but resumed baseball activities two days later. (Last updated: Feb. 27)
Down 4-6 weeks minimum before re-evaluation. (Last updated: Feb. 21)
Took a comebacker off the bat of Kyle Higashioka in batting practice and will be out for about four weeks. (Last updated: Feb. 21)
Has thrown multiple bullpens since the start of camp. Should be ready for a Minor League rehab assignment as the 2026 season opens. (Last updated: Feb. 20)
Continuing to rehab a shoulder injury that kept him sidelined for much of 2025. Should begin throwing bullpens before the end of camp. (Last updated: Feb. 20)
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline. (Last updated: Feb. 20)
Continuing to rehab a back injury that kept him sidelined for much of 2025. Behind in his throwing progression, but should return by the start of the Triple-A season. (Last updated: Feb. 20)

141.status: Popped up when Berríos underwent an MRI for the WBC. A follow-up MRI showed "different inflammation" from a year ago. No ligaments are involved; Berríos will keep throwing for now.

Tiedemann was scheduled to begin throwing again March 10, which is a step in the right direction after being shut down the past couple of weeks. The Blue Jays were working to find some preventative measures to help Tiedemann avoid further elbow soreness in the future.

141.expectedReturn: Week to week with a "delayed" ramp-up
141.status: Bieber working in a couple of "heavy" throw days each week, but has yet to return to the mound and it will remain difficult to chart a timeline until that happens.
Garcia is on a similar timeline to Bieber, working through some "heavy" throw days each week, but he has not thrown off a mound yet.
Santander underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Francis underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season. He is with the club now to begin his rehab in Florida.

142.status: Progressing through workouts and has begun throwing a medicine ball, focusing on shoulder strength, with no issues. Hopes to resume throwing the week of March 16. (Last updated: March 12)
Has resumed normal workouts and hopes to resume throwing by the end of the March 13-15 weekend. (Last updated: March 12)
Threw a bullpen session on March 11 and plans to throw another on March 13. (Last updated: March 12)

142.playerName: Walker Jenkins (MIN No. 1 prospect, No. 14 overall)
142.status: Suffered injury on Feb. 28 and will be held out “for a bit.” (Last updated: March 1)

142.injury: “Significant tearing” in UCL in right elbow
142.status: Underwent surgery on Feb. 25 to repair, rather than reconstruct, the UCL and will miss the season. (Last updated: Feb. 26) More >

143.status: Threw his fifth bullpen session on March 10, throwing approximately 46 pitches over two simulated innings. (Last updated: March 10)
Threw a live BP on March 10. Will throw another live BP on March 14. Remains on course to make the Opening Day roster. (Last updated: March 10)
Had not played this spring before being optioned to Minor League camp on March 7. Had a PRP injection in the offseason, but he remains sidelined. (Last updated: March 10)
Left Team Israel in the World Baseball Classic after feeling something in his abdomen/oblique. Phillies manager Rob Thomson said it’s “very mild.” (Last updated: March 10)

Sent to Philadelphia on March 6 to see another team doctor about his recurring back issue. Manager Rob Thomson said there is zero concern and the team is being "super cautious." There is no timetable for return to action. (Last updated: March 7)

144.status: Murphy took batting practice on the field on March 9. Atlanta is hoping the 2023 All-Star catcher returns from hip surgery during the first half of May. (Last updated: March 10)
Wentz will miss the 2026 season after a tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8. (Last updated: March 9)

Waldrep underwent arthroscopic surgery on Feb. 23 to have loose bodies removed from his right elbow. He could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on his return. (Last updated: Feb. 23)

Schwellenbach underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on his return. (Last updated: Feb. 18)
Jiménez missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return. (Last updated: Feb. 10)
Underwent surgery on his right elbow on June 9. (Last updated: Sept. 28)

147.status: Is hitting overhand tosses and could advance to live at-bats on or around April 1. Volpe is participating in all defensive and baserunning drills and began diving on his left shoulder in early March. Underwent arthroscopic surgery Oct. 12.

147.injury: Loose bodies, bone spur in left elbow
147.status: Tossed 20 pitches in live batting practice on March 9, marking his first session against live hitters since October elbow surgery. Rodón could appear in at least one Grapefruit League game and is expected to be built between 40-50 pitches by Opening Day. Manager Aaron Boone indicated March 9 that an April return remains in play.

Was facing hitters and throwing breaking balls as of March 6, when he threw two innings of live batting practice. Fastball has touched 97.5 mph. Could participate in Grapefruit League games this spring but will not be ready for Opening Day.
Tossing at distances of up to 90 feet on flat ground as of Feb. 27. Tentatively scheduled to begin throwing bullpens in late March. Could face hitters in April or May.
Recovering from February 2025 procedure. Has thrown eight bullpens as of mid-February. Said Feb. 14 he is not sure when game action will begin. "I'm taking it slow right now."

158.status: Scheduled to see a specialist in Dallas on March 12 for more information about the source of his lingering discomfort. Brewers are still hopeful about an April return.
Listed as probable starter March 9, but reported mild soreness in same elbow that landed him on IL in 2025. Mound session scheduled March 12. Potential game action March 15.
Injured running to first base on March 9. Was scheduled to see Dr. William Raasch on March 11 to chart a comeback.
Suffered Grade 1 quad strain legging out a triple against the Padres on Feb. 23. Expected back in action on March 12 against the Guardians, per manager Pat Murphy.
Being treated for a subscapularis strain.
A non-roster invitee to big league camp, Garabito had a bone fragment removed and a tendon repaired in his foot. He's expected to miss about four months.
A former top prospect who was in camp as a non-roster invitee, Garcia was injured playing winter ball.
